CENTRAL


| A B 0
FAV0RS

ENDORSES INITIATIVE
MOVEMENT FOR NEW


ORDINANCE.

pages.raw_text:
BY unanimous vote of the Cen
trial Labor union last night It was
aereeo to support the initiative
movement looking to the adoption
Of street speaking ordinance
that will settle the free speech
controversy between the city and
the Industrial Workers OF the
WorlG.


The petitions are being printed
this afternoon aud will be placed
in general circulation tonight. The
names of members of the different
unions willing to take out thn pc
titions have been secured AMI
there will be an effort made to
get the necessary 20 per cent of
the voters without unnecessary
delay.


The proposed ordinance, which
gives the right to speak on the
street under police regulations,
was Endorsed. as fair and reason
able by the representatives of the
different unions present at. the
meeting of the Central body last
night. Fred II. Moore, the attor-
ney who framed the ordinance,
was present and explained its
meaning.
